stmt: ∀ {Γ Δ : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {A B : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp}, (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Δ A → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.ldiv A B) → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Δ ++ Γ) B) ∧ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Γ (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.rdiv B A) → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent Δ A → Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent (Γ ++ Δ) B)
priority_rationale: This is a useful application/elimination lemma, but it looks downstream of the residuation correspondences and mainly shortens nearby proof paths instead of changing the top-level picture.

stmt: ∀ {A : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp} {s : String}, Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent [A] (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom s) → A = Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Tp.atom s
priority_rationale: Singleton-to-atom rigidity is a neat boundary fact, yet it is narrowly formulated and does not currently connect separate theory clusters or yield a broad characterization.
As a single shallow left counterexample candidate, it helps boundary detection but remains a one-off instance unless lifted into a broader separability or admissibility pattern.
This right-shallow non-derivability candidate has the same issue as the left-shallow one: useful only as isolated evidence unless it supports a wider structural separation theorem.
